exercises.prompt: Empareja cada palabra con su traducción

exercises.q: We ___ for the train since 4 o'clock, and it still hasn't arrived.
exercises.hint: «Since 4 o'clock» marca el inicio de un proceso inconcluso.
exercises.explanation: «Since 4 o'clock» señala el inicio de un proceso que sigue sin terminar: Present Perfect Continuous. «Are waiting» no combina con «since» y «have waited» no enfatiza el proceso frustrante.

exercises.text: I ___ been studying English for three hours.
exercises.answer: have
exercises.explanation: El sujeto «I» concuerda con «have», no con «has» ni «am».

exercises.q: Choose the correctly formed affirmative sentence.
options: It has been raining all day.
It has be raining all day.
exercises.hint: El participio «been» nunca desaparece ni cambia de forma.
exercises.explanation: La estructura fija es «have/has + been + verbo-ing». «Been» nunca se omite ni se sustituye por «be».

exercises.text: She ___ been working on this project for two weeks.
exercises.answer: has
exercises.explanation: El sujeto «she» (tercera persona singular) concuerda con «has».

exercises.q: Which sentence correctly shows the DURATION of a process?
options: I have run for two hours today.
I am running for two hours today.
I have been running for two hours today.
exercises.hint: Se busca la estructura idónea para enfatizar el proceso en curso.
exercises.explanation: Present Perfect Continuous es la estructura idónea para enfatizar la duración de un proceso en curso: «have been running».

exercises.text: They ___ been waiting for the bus for thirty minutes.
exercises.answer: have
exercises.explanation: El sujeto plural «they» concuerda con «have».

exercises.prompt: He estado trabajando durante tres horas
exercises.answer: I have been working for three hours
exercises.explanation: «Have been working» + «for» + período de tiempo expresa la duración de un proceso.

exercises.prompt: Ella ha estado corriendo toda la mañana
exercises.answer: She has been running all morning
exercises.explanation: Tercera persona singular: «has been running».

exercises.answer: We have been waiting for the progress
exercises.explanation: Sujeto plural «we» concuerda con «have been waiting».

exercises.text: I have been studying English for three hours.
It has been raining all day.
exercises.es: Ha estado lloviendo todo el día.

exercises.text: We have been waiting for the bus for thirty minutes.
exercises.es: Llevamos treinta minutos esperando el autobús.

exercises.prompt: Empareja cada palabra con su traducción

exercises.q: Your friend arrives sweating and breathing heavily. What do you ask?
exercises.hint: El efecto físico visible es la pista del proceso reciente.
exercises.explanation: «Have you been running?» pregunta por la actividad reciente cuyo efecto físico es visible ahora mismo.

exercises.text: You look ___; have you been running?
exercises.answer: exhausted
exercises.explanation: «Exhausted» (agotado) describe el cansancio extremo visible tras correr.

exercises.q: The kitchen is messy and there are dirty pans everywhere. What's the best explanation?
options: My brother has been cooking dinner.
exercises.hint: El desorden visible apunta a un proceso prolongado, no a un resultado ni a una acción instantánea.
exercises.explanation: El desorden visible es la prueba de una actividad reciente y prolongada: «has been cooking».

exercises.text: I am tired because I've ___ books all night.
exercises.answer: been reading
exercises.explanation: Falta «been» para completar la estructura del Present Perfect Continuous: «I've been reading».

exercises.q: Which sentence correctly explains why someone's hands are covered in paint?
options: She has been painting the wall.
exercises.hint: El efecto visible ahora mismo es consecuencia de un proceso reciente.
exercises.explanation: La evidencia física reciente (manos manchadas) se explica con el proceso: «has been painting».

exercises.text: Your hands are dirty. What ___ doing?
exercises.answer: have you been
exercises.explanation: La pregunta sobre la actividad reciente con efecto visible usa «have you been» + gerundio.

exercises.answer: You are sweating, have you been running
exercises.explanation: El efecto visible (sudar) se explica preguntando por el proceso reciente: «have you been running».

exercises.prompt: La cocina está sucia porque ha estado cocinando
exercises.answer: The kitchen is dirty because she has been cooking
exercises.explanation: «Has been cooking» explica el desorden visible como consecuencia de un proceso reciente.

exercises.prompt: Estoy agotado porque he estado pintando
exercises.answer: I am exhausted because I have been painting
exercises.explanation: «Have been painting» explica el cansancio actual como resultado de un proceso reciente.

exercises.text: You are sweating. Have you been running?
My brother has been cooking dinner; the kitchen is messy.
exercises.es: Mi hermano ha estado cocinando la cena; la cocina está desordenada.

exercises.text: I am exhausted because I have been painting all day.
exercises.es: Estoy agotado porque he estado pintando todo el día.

sublevels.title: Preguntas, negativas y la trampa de 's

exercises.prompt: Empareja cada palabra con su traducción

exercises.q: You look completely exhausted! What ___ doing?
exercises.hint: Necesitas la inversión sujeto-auxiliar para preguntar.
exercises.explanation: El estado presente («exhausted») es evidencia física de una actividad continua reciente; se necesita la inversión «have you been» + gerundio.

exercises.text: She ___ been working on the project recently.
exercises.answer: hasn't
exercises.explanation: Negativa de tercera persona singular: «hasn't been».

exercises.q: The kitchen is very messy and dirty because my brother ___ a cake.
exercises.hint: El desorden visible es prueba de un proceso, no solo de un resultado.
exercises.explanation: El desorden es la prueba visual de una actividad reciente y continuada: «has been cooking».

exercises.text: He's ___ running in the park.
exercises.answer: is
exercises.explanation: «He's running» (sin «been») es Present Continuous: el «'s» equivale a «is». Solo con «He's been running» el «'s» equivaldría a «has».

exercises.q: Which question correctly asks about a recent painting activity with visible effects?
options: Did he paint the living room?
Is he painted the living room?
Has he been painting the living room?
exercises.hint: Se pregunta por un proceso reciente con efectos visibles ahora.
exercises.explanation: La pregunta sobre un proceso reciente con efectos visibles usa la inversión «has he been» + gerundio.

exercises.text: They haven't ___ practicing the play for the school show.
exercises.answer: been
exercises.explanation: La negativa mantiene «been» invariable: «haven't been practicing».

exercises.answer: Have you been cleaning the house
exercises.explanation: Pregunta con inversión: «Have you been cleaning».

exercises.prompt: No ha estado nevando esta semana
exercises.answer: It hasn't been snowing this week
exercises.explanation: Negativa de tercera persona: «hasn't been snowing».

exercises.answer: It smells weird, what have you been cooking
exercises.explanation: El olor es la evidencia presente que motiva la pregunta por el proceso reciente: «have you been cooking».

exercises.text: Have you been cleaning the house? It smells nice.
exercises.es: ¿Has estado limpiando la casa? Huele bien.

exercises.text: It's been raining, and the streets are wet.
exercises.es: Ha estado lloviendo, y las calles están mojadas.

exercises.text: What have you been cooking? It smells amazing.

sublevels.title: For y Since: duración vs. punto de inicio

exercises.prompt: Empareja cada palabra con su traducción

exercises.q: She has ___ three different languages since she started her training program.
exercises.explanation: «Been learning» describe un proceso activo iniciado en el pasado que continúa hoy; encaja con el marcador «since».

exercises.text: He has been training for the marathon ___ January.
exercises.answer: since
exercises.explanation: «Since» se usa con un punto de inicio específico, como un mes.

exercises.q: Choose the sentence with the correct preposition for a period of time.
options: I have been practicing since three hours.
I have been practicing for three hours.
I have been practicing from three hours.
exercises.hint: «Three hours» es un intervalo de duración, no un punto de inicio.
exercises.explanation: «Three hours» es un período de duración, así que exige «for», no «since» ni «from».

exercises.text: We have been ___ the bus for thirty minutes.
exercises.answer: waiting for
exercises.explanation: «Wait for» es una expresión fija; en la forma continua se usa «waiting for».

exercises.q: Which stative verb CANNOT be used in the continuous form, even with duration?
exercises.hint: Uno de los tres describe un estado mental, no una acción.
exercises.explanation: «Know» es un verbo de estado mental y nunca acepta la forma continua, a diferencia de «study» y «practice», que son acciones.

exercises.text: She has been ___ for a new job since March.
exercises.answer: searching for
exercises.explanation: Present Perfect Continuous: «has been» + gerundio + preposición fija «searching for».

exercises.prompt: Ha estado estudiando tres idiomas desde marzo
exercises.answer: She has been studying three languages since March
exercises.explanation: «Since March» marca el punto de inicio del proceso: «has been studying since March».

exercises.prompt: Hemos estado practicando durante dos horas
exercises.answer: We have been practicing for two hours
exercises.explanation: «For two hours» indica la duración: «have been practicing for two hours».

exercises.answer: He has been training since January
exercises.explanation: «Since January» marca el punto de inicio del entrenamiento.

exercises.text: She has been learning three languages since she started her training.
exercises.es: Ha estado aprendiendo tres idiomas desde que empezó su formación.

exercises.text: We have been practicing for two hours.
I have been searching for a new job since March.
exercises.es: Llevo desde marzo buscando un trabajo nuevo.

sublevels.title: Continuo vs. Simple: proceso, cantidad y verbos de estado

exercises.prompt: Empareja cada palabra con su traducción

exercises.q: Which sentence focuses on the RESULT, not the process?
options: I've been written a letter to my boss.
I've been writing a letter to my boss.
I've written a letter to my boss.
exercises.hint: Una opción es agramatical y otra enfatiza el proceso, no el resultado.
exercises.explanation: «I've written» (Simple) se centra en el resultado: la carta está terminada. «I've been writing» enfatizaría el proceso, y «I've been written» es agramatical.

exercises.text: She has ___ three walls today.
exercises.answer: painted
exercises.explanation: «How many» (cantidad completada) exige Present Perfect Simple: «has painted three walls».

exercises.q: I ___ my colleague for five years, and she is a wonderful person.
exercises.explanation: «Know» es un verbo de estado y nunca acepta la forma continua; con duración («for five years») se usa Present Perfect Simple: «have known».

exercises.text: She has been ___ for her keys for ten minutes.
exercises.answer: looking
exercises.explanation: Proceso en curso: «has been looking for».

exercises.q: Which sentence correctly focuses on the DURATION of an ongoing activity?
options: He has been watching this show for two hours.
He has watched this show two times.
He watches this show every day.
exercises.hint: Se pregunta por «how long», no por «how many».
exercises.explanation: «For two hours» responde a «how long»; el proceso en curso se expresa con Present Perfect Continuous: «has been watching».

exercises.text: I have ___ this book three times this year.
exercises.answer: read
exercises.explanation: «Three times» indica cantidad, no duración, así que corresponde Present Perfect Simple: «have read».

exercises.prompt: He estado leyendo este libro toda la tarde
exercises.answer: I have been reading this book all afternoon
exercises.explanation: «Have been reading» enfatiza el proceso continuo de lectura durante la tarde.

exercises.answer: She has written three letters today
exercises.explanation: «Three letters» es una cantidad completada, así que corresponde Present Perfect Simple: «has written».

exercises.prompt: La conozco desde hace cinco años
exercises.answer: I have known her for five years
exercises.explanation: «Know» es un verbo de estado: solo admite Present Perfect Simple, «have known», nunca la forma continua.

exercises.text: I've been writing a letter, but I haven't finished it yet.
exercises.es: He estado escribiendo una carta, pero todavía no la he terminado.

exercises.text: She has painted three walls today.
I have known my colleague for five years.
exercises.es: Conozco a mi colega desde hace cinco años.
